# Parent AI — llms.txt
# https://www.parent-ai.eu

## About

Parent AI is a blog by Julien Seurat, a French father documenting his experiences teaching artificial intelligence to his two children (Romane, 6, and Meryl, 3) in a hands-on, playful, screen-free way.

Each article describes a real activity tested with the family, the children's reactions (failures included), and a simple explanation of one AI concept.

## Topics covered

- Classification and categorization
- Memory and machine learning
- Pattern recognition
- Natural language and comprehension
- Logic and decision-making
- Creativity and generation

## Content structure

Each article follows this structure:
1. In medias res opening (a concrete scene)
2. AI concept explained simply
3. Step-by-step activity (materials, duration, how it went)
4. What really happened (children's reactions, successes and failures)
5. An open question or observation to close

## Audience

Parents, educators, and anyone who wants to explain AI to children aged 4 to 10 without screens or technical jargon.

## Useful links

- Home: https://www.parent-ai.eu
- Blog: https://www.parent-ai.eu/blog
- About: https://www.parent-ai.eu/about
- Sitemap: https://www.parent-ai.eu/sitemap.xml
- RSS: https://www.parent-ai.eu/feed.xml

## Author

Julien Seurat — father, tech enthusiast
